```{r}
          mh_lambda_0.75_chain_4$rs$samples),
                  top_k=149,
    epsilon=1e-20,
    alpha=0.1)

plot_inclusion(inclusion_summary_lam_0.75_asy_CI)
```


```{r}
incl.summary.v2$variable
```



# Attempted empirical rule for choosing lambda.


```{r}

bic_max_min_diff = function(K=1000, bic_burnin=1000,
                            chain_id=3, max_min_diff=FALSE){
  
  
  ### note burn-in has not been applied to BIC traces 

  # extract all RS results
  pattern = sprintf("^mh_lambda_[0-9\\.]+_chain_%d_",chain_id)
  files <- list.files(
    here("output", "mh_runs_4_lambda_run_2"),
    pattern = pattern, full.names = TRUE
  )
  
  # extract lambda values
  lambdas <- na.omit(str_extract(list.files(here("output", "mh_runs_4_lambda_run_2")), ".*mh_lambda_(.+)_chain_3", group=1))
  lambdas = sort(unique(as.numeric(lambdas)))
  #print(lambdas)
  
  empirical_criteria = c()
  
  
  i=1
  for (f in files){
    obj <- readRDS(f)
    
    #cat(sprintf("\nRead file: %s \n", f))
    # Read BIC traces, remove unaccounted burnin, and after that
    # take first K  BIC values for empirical rule
    
    bt  <- obj$rs$bic_trace[bic_burnin : (K+bic_burnin)]
    min_bic = min(bt)
    max_bic = max(bt)
    
    if (max_min_diff){
      cat(sprintf("For lam=%.2f, max_bic-min_bic=%.3f\n",
                lambdas[i], max_bic-min_bic))
      # obtain normalized difference between smallest and greatest BIC
      # exp(-lambda*BIC_min) / (exp(-lambda*BIC_min) + exp(-lambda*BIC_max))
      # = 1 / (1 + exp(-lambda*BIC_max + lambda*BIC_min))
      # = 1 / (1 + exp(-lambda * (BIC_max - BIC_min))
      # = plogis(lambda*(BIC_max - BIC_min))
      empirical_criteria[i] =  stats::plogis(lambdas[i]*(max_bic - min_bic))
    } else {
      empirical_criteria[i] =  min_bic
    }
    
    i = i + 1
    
  }
  names(empirical_criteria) = lambdas
  sort(empirical_criteria)

     
}
  
#bic_max_min_diff()

```

```{r}
bic_max_min_diff(K=3000, max_min_diff=TRUE)
```

```{r}
bic_max_min_diff(K=2000)
```

```{r}
bic_max_min_diff(K=3000)
```


```{r}
bic_max_min_diff(K=5000)
```


# RS on core vars?

```{r}
Gibbs.on.core.vars = Gibbs.random.search.lambda(
  v0=rep(1, length(varnames_elites[[1]])),
  data=data.frame(X[,varnames_elites[[1]]], y),
  lambda=3,
  debug=TRUE
)
```


```{r}
MH.on.elite.vars = MH.random.search(
  #v0=rep(1,length(varnames_elites[[1]])),
  v0=tail(mh_lambda_0.75_chain_3$rs$samples, n=1),
  data=data.frame(X, y),
  lambda=0.75,
  debug=TRUE
)
```


```{r}
Gibbs.on.elite.vars = Gibbs.random.search.lambda(
  #v0=rep(1,length(varnames_elites[[1]])),
  v0=rbinom(length(varnames_elites[[1]]), size=1, prob=0.5),
  data=data.frame(X[,varnames_elites[[1]]], y),
  lambda=1.5,
  debug=TRUE
)
```


# Try compute BIC for marginals > 0.5?


```{r}

vars_lam0.75_gt_0.5 = (inclusion_summary_lam_0.75 %>% filter(p_hat >= 0.5))$variable

for (k in 1:length(vars_lam0.75_gt_0.5)){
  bic_first_k_rel_freqs = compute.BIC.auto(data.frame(X, y),
                 colnames(X) %in% vars_lam0.75_gt_0.5[1:k]
                 )
  cat(sprintf("BIC for first %d vars with highest rel freqs: %.3f\n", k, bic_first_k_rel_freqs))
}




```
# Proportion of top k marginal freqs containing core vars

```{r}

for (k in length(core_vars_among_elites):nrow(inclusion_summary_lam_0.75)){
     how_many_top_ks_match_cores = length(
              intersect(
                (inclusion_summary_lam_0.75$variable)[1:k],
                core_vars_among_elites
              )
     )
     
    cat(sprintf("Proportion of Top %d marginal freqs matching core vars: %.3f (i.e. %d out of %d) \n",
                k, how_many_top_ks_match_cores/length(core_vars_among_elites),
                how_many_top_ks_match_cores, length(core_vars_among_elites))
        )

    if (how_many_top_ks_match_cores == length(core_vars_among_elites)){
      print(k)
      print((inclusion_summary_lam_0.75$variable)[1:k])
      break
    }
}



```

```{r}

setdiff(
    core_vars_among_elites, 
    (inclusion_summary_lam_0.75$variable)[1:41]
)
```


# Stepwise?
```{r}
basemod = glm(y ~ 1, data=data.frame(X,y), family=binomial)
stepmod = step(basemod,
               scope=as.formula(paste("~ ", paste(colnames(X), collapse= "+"))),
               k=log(nrow(X)))
```

## Compute its BIC value

```{r}
#saveRDS(stepmod,
#        here("output", "validation_outputs", "stepmod.rds"))
```


```{r}

compute.BIC.auto(data.frame(X,y),
                 colnames(X) %in% names(stepmod$coefficients))


```
## Compute CV log loss, AUCPR

```{r}
cv_logloss(X, y, colnames(X) %in% names(stepmod$coefficients))
```
```{r}
cv_aucpr(X, y, colnames(X) %in% names(stepmod$coefficients))
```

```{r}
cv_deviance_stepwise = cv_deviance_rs(X, y,
                     vars = names(stepmod$coefficients)[!names(stepmod$coefficients) %in% c("(Intercept)")],
                     foldid = foldid)
cv_deviance_stepwise
```


# Proportion of top k marginal freqs containing core vars

```{r}



for (k in length(varnames_elites[[25]]):nrow(inclusion_summary_lam_0.75)){
     how_many_top_ks_match_best_elites = length(
              intersect(
                (inclusion_summary_lam_0.75$variable)[1:k],
                varnames_elites[[25]]
              )
     )
     
    cat(sprintf("Proportion of Top %d marginal freqs matching best elite vars: %.3f (i.e. %d out of %d) \n",
                k, how_many_top_ks_match_best_elites/length(varnames_elites[[25]]),
                how_many_top_ks_match_best_elites, length(varnames_elites[[25]]))
        )

    if (how_many_top_ks_match_best_elites == length(varnames_elites[[25]])){
      print(k)
      print((inclusion_summary_lam_0.75$variable)[1:k])
      break
    }
}



```

```{r}

compute.BIC.auto(
  data.frame(X,y),
  colnames(X) %in% (inclusion_summary_lam_0.75 %>% filter(p_hat >= 0.5))$variable
) / 2


```

```{r}

compute.BIC.auto(
  data.frame(X,y),
  core_vars_among_elites
) /2


```



```{r}
p_chart_slowest_converging_vars(mh_lambda_0.75_chain_1$rs$samples,
                                metric='MCSE')
```


# Top pressure-level core variables

```{r}
compute.BIC.auto(data.frame(X,y),
                 colnames(X) %in%
                   c("Coriolis_parameter_absolute",
                     "vorticity_relative_at_700_hPa",
                     "medium_cloud_cover",
                     "wind_u_at_1000_hPa",
                     "divergence",
                     "vertical_velocity_at_700_hPa",
                     "potential_vorticity_at_850_hPa",
                     "temperature",
                     "geopotential_at_500_hPa",
                     "vertical_wind_shear_between_925_hPa_and_1000_hPa",
                     "potential_vorticity_at_400_hPa",
                     "wind_v_at_200_hPa",
                     "sea_surface_temperature",
                     "vorticity_relative_at_200_hPa",
                     "vertical_wind_shear_between_200_hPa_and_850_hPa",
                     "wind_u_at_925_hPa",
                     "vorticity_relative_at_850_hPa",
                     "specific_humidity_at_200_hPa",
                     "divergence_at_700_hPa"))
```



```{r}
cv_logloss(X, y,
           vars=c("Coriolis_parameter_absolute",
                     "vorticity_relative_at_700_hPa",
                     "medium_cloud_cover",
                     "wind_u_at_1000_hPa",
                     "divergence",
                     "vertical_velocity_at_700_hPa",
                     "potential_vorticity_at_850_hPa",
                     "temperature",
                     "geopotential_at_500_hPa",
                     "vertical_wind_shear_between_925_hPa_and_1000_hPa",
                     "potential_vorticity_at_400_hPa",
                     "wind_v_at_200_hPa",
                     "sea_surface_temperature",
                     "vorticity_relative_at_200_hPa",
                     "vertical_wind_shear_between_200_hPa_and_850_hPa",
                     "wind_u_at_925_hPa",
                     "vorticity_relative_at_850_hPa",
                     "specific_humidity_at_200_hPa",
                     "divergence_at_700_hPa"))
```



# Claim: non-core variables having p_hat >= 0.5 are just included in suboptimal models.

```{r}
non_core_vars_phat_gt_0.5 = (inclusion_summary_lam_0.75 %>%
  filter(!variable %in% core_vars_among_elites) %>%
  filter(p_hat >= 0.5))$variable
```

```{r}
(inclusion_summary_lam_0.75 %>%
  filter(!variable %in% core_vars_among_elites) %>%
  filter(p_hat >= 0.5))
```







```{r}

#non_core_vars_phat_gt_0.5 = (inclusion_summary_lam_0.75 %>%
#  filter(!variable %in% core_vars_among_elites) %>%
#  filter(p_hat >= 0.5))$variable


#mh_lambda_0.75_chains = list(
#  mh_lambda_0.75_chain_1,
#  mh_lambda_0.75_chain_2,
#  mh_lambda_0.75_chain_3,
#  mh_lambda_0.75_chain_4
#)

n_chains <- length(mh_lambda_0.75_chains)
burn     <- 1000L

#chain_idxs = seq_len(n_chains)
chain_idxs = c(3)

for (non_core_var in non_core_vars_phat_gt_0.5) {
  for (chain_idx in chain_idxs) {
    chains <- mh_lambda_0.75_chains[[chain_idx]]
    cat(sprintf("\nInvestigating %s in chain %d\n", non_core_var, chain_idx))

    # post-burn BIC; check alignment
    bic_post <- chains$rs$bic_trace[(burn+1):length(chains$rs$bic_trace)]
    stopifnot(nrow(chains$rs$samples) == length(bic_post))

    # obtain indices
    sel_idx <- which(chains$rs$samples[, non_core_var] == 1L)

    if (length(sel_idx) == 0L) {
      prop_near <- 0
    } else {
      # choose your baseline: global best or best at this λ
      delta <- bic_post[sel_idx] - best_BIC_minimizer_all_lambda$best$BIC
      
      # HOW many distinct near optimal models among all models containing non_core_var
      n_distinct_nearoptimal_mods = length(
        unique(
          apply(chains$rs$samples[which(delta <= 2), ],
            MARGIN=1,
            function(x)paste0(x,collapse=""))
        )
      )
      # HOW many distinct models containing non_core_var
      n_distinct_models = length(
        unique(
          apply(chains$rs$samples[sel_idx, ],
            MARGIN=1,
            function(x)paste0(x,collapse=""))
        )
      )
      
      prop_near <- n_distinct_nearoptimal_mods / n_distinct_models
    }
    cat(sprintf("Proportion near-optimal (ΔBIC ≤ 2): %.4f (i.e. %d DISTINCT near-optimal models out of %d DISTINCT models)\n",
                prop_near, n_distinct_nearoptimal_mods, n_distinct_models))
  }
  cat("----------------------------------------------------------\n")
}

```
# Plot core vars for presentation

```{r}
inclusion_summary_lam_0.75 %>%
  filter(variable %in% core_vars_among_elites)
```


## Sort by MAXIMUM p_hat for @ family

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# 1. Filter for your core variables
core_tbl <- inclusion_summary_lam_0.75 %>%
  filter(variable %in% core_vars_among_elites)

# 2. Extract base variable (physical field) and pressure level label
core_tbl <- core_tbl %>%
  mutate(
    base = case_when(
      str_detect(variable, "vertical_wind_shear_between_") ~ "vertical_wind_shear",
      str_detect(variable, "_at_") ~ str_replace(variable, "_at_.*$", ""),
      TRUE ~ variable
    ),
    level = case_when(
      str_detect(variable, "_between_") ~ str_replace(variable, ".*_between_", "") |> str_replace_all("_", " "),
      str_detect(variable, "_at_") ~ str_replace(variable, ".*_at_", "") |> str_replace_all("_", " "),
      TRUE ~ "physical"
    )
  )

# 3. Compute max p_hat for each base variable
leaders <- core_tbl %>%
  group_by(base) %>%
  summarise(max_phat_base = max(p_hat, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(max_phat_base), base) %>%
  mutate(base_rank = row_number())

# 4. Join base ranking back to the full table
ordered_tbl <- core_tbl %>%
  left_join(leaders, by = "base") %>%
  arrange(base_rank, desc(p_hat), level) %>%
  select(base, level, p_hat, ess, se, lower, upper, variable, base_rank)


```

```{r}
ordered_tbl
```

## Take maxs only 
```{r}
winners_only <- core_tbl %>%
  group_by(base) %>%
  filter(p_hat == max(p_hat, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(leaders %>% mutate(base_rank = row_number()), by = c("base","p_hat"="max_phat_base")) %>%
  arrange(base_rank, level) %>%
  select(base, level, p_hat, variable)

```

```{r}
winners_only
```

## Begin plotting!


```{r}

#install.packages('ggtext')

# ---- packages ----
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggtext)      # to render bold labels on the y-axis
# ordered_tbl, winners_only, core_coefs_df are assumed to exist (as in your last message)

# ---- 1) decide which winners to emphasize (or pass your own subset) ----
emphasize_vars <- winners_only$variable       # <- or provide a character vector yourself

# ---- 2) sign summary for winners from coefficient draws across elite models ----
sign_map <- core_coefs_df %>%
  filter(term %in% winners_only$variable) %>%
  group_by(variable = term) %>%
  summarise(min_est = min(estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
            max_est = max(estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(sign_cat = case_when(
    min_est > 0 ~ "positive",
    max_est < 0 ~ "negative",
    TRUE        ~ "mixed"
  ))

# nice, desaturated palette
pal <- c(positive = "#78A9CF",   # muted blue
         negative = "#E07B73",   # muted red
         mixed    = "#B8A56A")   # muted brown/olive
gray_bar <- "#D0D0D0"

# ---- 3) mark winners, attach sign category, and make plotting labels ----
plot_df <- ordered_tbl %>%
  mutate(is_winner = variable %in% emphasize_vars) %>%
  left_join(sign_map, by = c("variable")) %>%
  mutate(fill_col = ifelse(is_winner, pal[sign_cat], gray_bar),
         # pretty left labels: base (+ level if not "physical")
         label = ifelse(level == "physical",
                        base,
                        paste0(base, " (", level, ")"))
  )

# ---- 4) boldface Gray’s six canonical predictors on the y-axis ----
grays_vars <- c(
  "Coriolis_parameter_absolute",
  "vorticity_relative_at_850_hPa",      # low-level rel. vorticity (choose the one you want)
  "vertical_wind_shear_between_200_hPa_and_850_hPa",  # deep-layer shear; adjust if you prefer 200–950
  "sea_surface_temperature",
  "equiv_pot_temperature_diff_between_surface_and_500_hPa",
  "relative_humidity_at_700_hPa"        # mid-tropospheric humidity
)

plot_df <- plot_df %>%
  mutate(label_md = ifelse(variable %in% grays_vars,
                           paste0("**", label, "**"),
                           label))

# ---- 5) y ordering: follow your base_rank then within-base p_hat desc ----
plot_df <- plot_df %>%
  arrange(base_rank, desc(p_hat)) %>%
  mutate(label_md = factor(label_md, levels = rev(unique(label_md))))  # rev for horizontal bars

# ---- 6) build plot ----
p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = p_hat, y = label_md)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = I(fill_col)), width = 0.8) +
  scale_x_continuous("Inclusion probability (p̂)", limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    y = NULL,
    title = "Core variable importance by physical family & pressure level",
    subtitle = "Colored bars = dominant level(s) per family; color shows coefficient sign across elite models"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(lineheight = 1.2),   # enables bold labels
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p


```




# DEBUGGIN PACKAGE: need >= 0.52 of xfun

```{r}
    all_packages <- installed.packages()
    package_names <- rownames(all_packages)

    packages_using_xfun <- character()

    for (pkg in package_names) {
      desc_file <- file.path(find.package(pkg), "DESCRIPTION")
      if (file.exists(desc_file)) {
        desc_content <- readLines(desc_file)
        if (any(grepl("xfun", desc_content, ignore.case = TRUE))) {
          packages_using_xfun <- c(packages_using_xfun, pkg)
        }
      }
    }

    print(packages_using_xfun)


```

```{r}
## 0) Restart R (in RStudio: Session > Restart R)

## 1) See where xfun is coming from
.libPaths()
find.package("xfun", quiet = TRUE)
packageVersion("xfun")

## 2) Remove ANY installed copies across all lib paths
for (p in .libPaths()) {
  ip <- rownames(installed.packages(lib.loc = p))
  if ("xfun" %in% ip) remove.packages("xfun", lib = p)
}

## 3) Install fresh (pick one)
install.packages("xfun", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org", dependencies = TRUE)
# Windows/macOS binary explicitly:
# install.packages("xfun", type = "binary", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")

## 4) Verify
packageVersion("xfun")   # should be >= "0.52"

```

## consider multi-level dimensions

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggtext)   # for bold strip labels

# --- 1) Mark winners (dominant level per family) and attach sign category ----
sign_by_var <- core_coefs_df %>%
  group_by(term) %>%
  summarize(
    pos = all(estimate > 0),
    neg = all(estimate < 0),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    variable = term,
    sign_cat = case_when(
      pos ~ "pos",
      neg ~ "neg",
      TRUE ~ "mix"
    )
  )

plot_df <- ordered_tbl %>%
  left_join(sign_by_var, by = "variable") %>%
  mutate(
    winner = variable %in% winners_only$variable,
    sign_cat = ifelse(winner, sign_cat, "other")   # gray for non-winners
  )

# --- 2) Bold Gray’s 6 canonical predictors in the facet strips ---------------
grays_six <- c(
  "Coriolis_parameter_absolute",
  "vorticity_relative",                        # family label (low-level: 200–850 hPa shows up)
  "vertical_wind_shear",
  "sea_surface_temperature",
  "equiv_pot_temperature_diff_between_surface_and_500_hPa",
  "relative_humidity"
)

# Build a custom labeller for the facet strips
strip_labs <- unique(plot_df$base)
strip_map  <- setNames(
  ifelse(strip_labs %in% grays_six,
         paste0("<b>", strip_labs, "</b>"),
         strip_labs),
  strip_labs
)

# --- 3) Factor order: families by your base_rank, levels in the order they appear
plot_df <- plot_df %>% 
  mutate(
    base  = reorder(base, base_rank),
    level = factor(level, levels = rev(unique(level)))  # reversed so top-to-bottom matches table
  )


```



```{r}
library(scales)

label_num <- function(x) sprintf("%.2f", x)   # 0.00–1.00 format

SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR=0.5
p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*p_hat, y = level, fill = sign_cat)) +
  # thinner bars
  geom_col(width = 0.38) +
  # value labels just outside the right edge of each bar
  geom_text(aes(label = label_num(p_hat)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.1, color = "grey20") +
  facet_grid(
    rows = vars(base),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",
    labeller = labeller(base = strip_map)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(pos="#5BA4D0", neg="#D06A6A", mix="#B69569", other="#D9D9D9"),
    breaks = c("pos","neg","mix"), labels = c("positive","negative","mixed"), name = NULL
  ) +
  # give extra space at right so labels fit; keep subtle vertical guides
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*max(plot_df$p_hat)),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.12))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    # soften vertical guides; remove x labels/ticks
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_line(color = "grey92", linewidth = 0.2),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.spacing.y    = unit(10, "pt"),
    strip.placement    = "outside",
    strip.background.y = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y.left  = element_markdown(
      angle = 0, hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.05, margin = margin(r = 10)
    ),
    legend.position = "left"
  )

p

```

```{r}

SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR=0.05
p <- ggplot(plot_df %>% filter(winner==TRUE),
            aes(x = SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*p_hat, y = level, fill = sign_cat)) +
  # thinner bars
  geom_col(width = 0.38) +
  # value labels just outside the right edge of each bar
  geom_text(aes(label = label_num(p_hat)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.1, color = "grey20") +
  facet_grid(
    rows = vars(base),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",
    labeller = labeller(base = strip_map)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(pos="#5BA4D0", neg="#D06A6A", mix="#B69569", other="#D9D9D9"),
    breaks = c("pos","neg","mix"), labels = c("positive","negative","mixed"), name = NULL
  ) +
  # give extra space at right so labels fit; keep subtle vertical guides
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*max(plot_df$p_hat)),
                     breaks = seq(0, SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*max(plot_df$p_hat), 0.1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.12))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    # soften vertical guides; remove x labels/ticks
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_line(color = "grey92", linewidth = 0.2),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.spacing.y    = unit(10, "pt"),
    strip.placement    = "outside",
    strip.background.y = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y.left  = element_markdown(
      angle = 0, hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.05, margin = margin(r = 5)
    ),
    legend.position = "left"
  )

p
```



```{r}

SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR=0.05
p <- ggplot(plot_df %>% filter(winner==TRUE & sign_cat=="pos"),
            aes(x = SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*p_hat, y = level, fill = sign_cat)) +
  # thinner bars
  geom_col(width = 0.38) +
  # value labels just outside the right edge of each bar
  geom_text(aes(label = label_num(p_hat)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.1, color = "grey20") +
  facet_grid(
    rows = vars(base),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",
    labeller = labeller(base = strip_map)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(pos="#5BA4D0", neg="#D06A6A", mix="#B69569", other="#D9D9D9"),
    breaks = c("pos","neg","mix"), labels = c("positive","negative","mixed"), name = NULL
  ) +
  # give extra space at right so labels fit; keep subtle vertical guides
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*max(plot_df$p_hat)),
                     breaks = seq(0, SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*max(plot_df$p_hat), 0.1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.12))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    # soften vertical guides; remove x labels/ticks
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_line(color = "grey92", linewidth = 0.2),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.spacing.y    = unit(10, "pt"),
    strip.placement    = "outside",
    strip.background.y = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y.left  = element_markdown(
      angle = 0, hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.05, margin = margin(r = 5)
    ),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p
```



```{r}

SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR=0.15
p <- ggplot(plot_df %>% filter(winner==TRUE & sign_cat=="neg"),
            aes(x = SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*p_hat, y = level, fill = sign_cat)) +
  # thinner bars
  geom_col(width = 0.38) +
  # value labels just outside the right edge of each bar
  geom_text(aes(label = label_num(p_hat)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 5, color = "grey20") +
  facet_grid(
    rows = vars(base),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",
    labeller = labeller(base = strip_map)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(pos="#5BA4D0", neg="#D06A6A", mix="#B69569", other="#D9D9D9"),
    breaks = c("pos","neg","mix"), labels = c("positive","negative","mixed"), name = NULL
  ) +
  # give extra space at right so labels fit; keep subtle vertical guides
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5),
                     breaks = seq(0, 0.5, 0.1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.12))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    # soften vertical guides; remove x labels/ticks
    #panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.05),
    #panel.grid.minor.x = element_line(color = "grey92", linewidth = 0.05),
    
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.spacing.y    = unit(10, "pt"),
    strip.placement    = "outside",
    strip.background.y = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y.left  = element_markdown(
      angle = 0, hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.05, margin = margin(r = 5)
    ),
    legend.position.inside = c(0.55, 0.5),
    legend.position = "inside"
  )

p

ggsave(here("output", "mh_runs_4_lambda_run_2_plots",
            "neg_core_vars.png"),
       p, width = 10, height = 9, units = "in", bg='white',
       scale=1.3 # Scale all plot elements (text, points, spacing) with the canvas
       )
```

```{r}

SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR=0.15
p <- ggplot(plot_df %>% filter(winner==TRUE & sign_cat=="pos"),
            aes(x = SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*p_hat, y = level, fill = sign_cat)) +
  # thinner bars
  geom_col(width = 0.38) +
  # value labels just outside the right edge of each bar
  geom_text(aes(label = label_num(p_hat)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 5, color = "grey20") +
  facet_grid(
    rows = vars(base),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",
    labeller = labeller(base = strip_map)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(pos="#5BA4D0", neg="#D06A6A", mix="#B69569", other="#D9D9D9"),
    breaks = c("pos","neg","mix"), labels = c("positive","negative","mixed"), name = NULL
  ) +
  # give extra space at right so labels fit; keep subtle vertical guides
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5),
                     breaks = seq(0, 0.5, 0.1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.12))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    # soften vertical guides; remove x labels/ticks
    #panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.05),
    #panel.grid.minor.x = element_line(color = "grey92", linewidth = 0.05),
    
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.spacing.y    = unit(10, "pt"),
    strip.placement    = "outside",
    strip.background.y = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y.left  = element_markdown(
      angle = 0, hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.05, margin = margin(r = 5)
    ),
    legend.position.inside = c(0.55, 0.5),
    legend.position = "inside"
  )

p

ggsave(here("output", "mh_runs_4_lambda_run_2_plots",
            "pos_core_vars.png"),
       p, width = 10, height = 9, units = "in", bg='white',
       scale=1.3 # Scale all plot elements (text, points, spacing) with the canvas
       )
```

```{r}

SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR=0.15
p <- ggplot(plot_df %>% filter(winner==TRUE & sign_cat=="mix"),
            aes(x = SCALE_DOWN_FACTOR*p_hat, y = level, fill = sign_cat)) +
  # thinner bars
  geom_col(width = 0.38) +
  # value labels just outside the right edge of each bar
  geom_text(aes(label = label_num(p_hat)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 5, color = "grey20") +
  facet_grid(
    rows = vars(base),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",
    labeller = labeller(base = strip_map)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(pos="#5BA4D0", neg="#D06A6A", mix="#B69569", other="#D9D9D9"),
    breaks = c("pos","neg","mix"), labels = c("positive","negative","mixed"), name = NULL
  ) +
  # give extra space at right so labels fit; keep subtle vertical guides
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5),
                     breaks = seq(0, 0.5, 0.1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.12))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    # soften vertical guides; remove x labels/ticks
    #panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.05),
    #panel.grid.minor.x = element_line(color = "grey92", linewidth = 0.05),
    
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),

    panel.spacing.y    = unit(10, "pt"),
    strip.placement    = "outside",
    strip.background.y = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y.left  = element_markdown(
      angle = 0, hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.05, margin = margin(r = 5)
    ),
    legend.position.inside = c(0.55, 0.5),
    legend.position = "inside"
  )

p

ggsave(here("output", "mh_runs_4_lambda_run_2_plots",
            "mixed_core_vars.png"),
       p, width = 10, height = 9, units = "in", bg='white',
       scale=1.3 # Scale all plot elements (text, points, spacing) with the canvas
       )
```






```{r}

```



# Investigate coef for equiv_pot?

```{r}
core_coefs_df %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "equiv")) %>% 
  arrange(desc(estimate))
```


```{r}
best_BIC_minimizer_all_lambda$elite$BIC
```


```{r}

mod_idx_equiv_pot_coefs_gt_0 = (core_coefs_df %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "equiv") & estimate > 0) %>% 
  arrange(desc(estimate)) )$model_id


vars_in_mod_equiv_pot_coefs_gt_0 = Reduce(intersect,varnames_elites[mod_idx_equiv_pot_coefs_gt_0])


mod_idx_equiv_pot_coefs_le_0 = (core_coefs_df %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "equiv") & estimate <= 0) %>% 
  arrange(desc(estimate)) )$model_id

vars_in_mod_idx_equiv_pot_coefs_le_0 = Reduce(intersect,varnames_elites[mod_idx_equiv_pot_coefs_le_0])

```

```{r}
sort(
  best_BIC_minimizer_all_lambda$elite$BIC[mod_idx_equiv_pot_coefs_gt_0]
)

```

```{r}
sort(
  best_BIC_minimizer_all_lambda$elite$BIC[mod_idx_equiv_pot_coefs_le_0]
)
```
```{r}
sort(
  best_BIC_minimizer_all_lambda$elite$k[mod_idx_equiv_pot_coefs_gt_0]
)
```

```{r}
sort(
  best_BIC_minimizer_all_lambda$elite$k[mod_idx_equiv_pot_coefs_le_0]
)
```

Test: is each model on the >0 coef *nested* on any model for the >0 coef?





# BIC density plot for presentaion
```{r}
ggplot(data.frame(BIC=unlist(S_list_MH)[unlist(S_list_MH) <= 9800]),
       aes(x=BIC)) +
  geom_density(fill='red', bw=1.2, alpha=0.1) +
  theme_classic() + 
  xlim(9650, 9740) +

  labs(title="Illustration of BIC distributions \n in high dimensional model spaces",
       caption = "Illustration of 160,000 BIC values from sampled models in a 149-variable space.\nEach peak corresponds to clusters of models with similar quality.") + 
  theme(
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, # Center the title
                                  color = "black",
                                  size = 16,
                                  face = "bold"),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 7.5, colour = "grey30", hjust = 0),
    plot.caption.position = "plot" )    # puts caption below panel
```


```{r}
ggplot(data.frame(BIC=unlist(S_list_MH)[unlist(S_list_MH) <= 9800]),
       aes(x=BIC)) +
  geom_density(fill='green', bw=4, alpha=0.1) +
  theme_classic() + 
  xlim(9660, 9740) +

  labs(title="Illustration of BIC distributions \n in low dimensional model spaces",
       caption = "Illustration of 160,000 BIC values from sampled models in a 149-variable space.") + 
  theme(
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, # Center the title
                                  color = "black",
                                  size = 16,
                                  face = "bold"),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 7.5, colour = "grey30", hjust = 0),
    plot.caption.position = "plot" )    # puts caption below panel
```











```{r}
library(ggplot2)

make_bic_panel <- function(x, title, fill_col, bw = NULL,
                           bic_min = NULL, delta2 = 2,
                           xlim = range(x, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  # clean
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  stopifnot(is.numeric(x), length(x) > 1)

  if (is.null(bic_min)) bic_min <- min(x, na.rm = TRUE)

  df <- data.frame(BIC = x)

  ggplot(df, aes(BIC)) +
    # ΔBIC band as annotate() to avoid “length 1” warnings
    annotate("rect",
             xmin = bic_min - delta2, xmax = bic_min,
             ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
             fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_density(fill = fill_col, colour = "black",
                 bw = bw, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = bic_min, linetype = 2, linewidth = 0.4) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = xlim, expand = c(0, 0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), breaks = NULL) +
    labs(title = title, x = "BIC", y = NULL) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
          axis.ticks = element_blank(),
          axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
          axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5))
}


# Example usage (replace x_easy/x_hard with your two BIC vectors or the same vector with different bw)
x <- unlist(S_list_MH)                     # your 160k BICs
#rng <- quantile(X, c(.01, .99), na.rm = TRUE)  # common x-range

rng <- c(9650, 9740)
p_easy <- make_bic_panel(x, "…low-dimensional feel", "#cfeecf",
                         bw = 4, xlim = rng)
p_hard <- make_bic_panel(x, "…high-dimensional feel", "#f6c7c7",
                         bw = 0.8, xlim = rng)


cowplot::plot_grid(p_easy, p_hard, ncol = 1, rel_heights = c(1,1))

```




```{r}
plot(X[,'geopotential_at_200_hPa'], X[,'geopotential_at_500_hPa'])
```


# Check mode


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)

# --- helpers ---------------------------------------------------------------
check_mode = function(){
      # turn a 0/1 vector into a compact key (for counting)
    .bin_key <- function(v) paste0(v, collapse = "")
    
    # compute Hamming distance between two 0/1 model vectors
    ham_dist <- function(a, b){
      cat(sprintf("calculating hamming distance of a,b with lengths %d and %d\n",length(a),length(b)))
      dist = sum(a != b)
      cat(sprintf("done calculating hamming distance\n"))
      dist
    }
    
    # return the mode model of a chain (post burn-in)
    mode_model <- function(chain, burn = 1000) {
      #S   <- chain$rs$samples[(burn+1):nrow(chain$rs$samples), , drop = FALSE]
      S   <- chain$rs$samples
      bic <- chain$rs$bic_trace[(burn+1):length(chain$rs$bic_trace)]  # aligns to S rows
      cat(sprintf("nrow(S)=%d, length(bic)=%d\n", nrow(S), length(bic)))
    
      keys <- apply(S, 1, .bin_key)
      tab  <- sort(table(keys), decreasing = TRUE)
      topk <- names(tab)[1]
      idx  <- which(keys == topk)
    
      v_mode <- as.integer(strsplit(topk, "")[[1]])
    
      list(
        key        = topk,
        v          = v_mode,
        count      = as.integer(tab[1]),
        prop       = as.integer(tab[1]) / nrow(S),
        bic_min    = min(bic[idx]),
        bic_median = median(bic[idx]),
        idx        = idx
      )
    }
    
    # convenience: summarize a chain against the global best
    summarize_chain_mode <- function(chain, chain_id, best) {
      mm <- mode_model(chain)
      tibble(
        chain   = chain_id,
        visits  = mm$count,
        prop    = mm$prop,
        bic_min = mm$bic_min,
        dBIC    = mm$bic_min - best$BIC,
        hamming_to_global_best = ham_dist(mm$v, colnames(X) %in% best$v),
        key     = mm$key
      )
    }
    
    # --- data you already have -------------------------------------------------
    # mh_lambda_0.75_chain_1 .. _4
    # best_BIC_minimizer_all_lambda$best$v and $BIC
    
    chains_075 <- list(
      `1: full`   = mh_lambda_0.75_chain_1,
      `2: empty`  = mh_lambda_0.75_chain_2,
      `3: sparse` = mh_lambda_0.75_chain_3,
      `4: random` = mh_lambda_0.75_chain_4
    )
    
    # --- main summaries --------------------------------------------------------
    
    mode_summaries <- imap_dfr(chains_075, ~ summarize_chain_mode(.x, .y, best_BIC_minimizer_all_lambda$best))
    
    mode_summaries
    # Columns:
    # chain | visits | prop | bic_min | dBIC | hamming_to_global_best | key
    
    # do all chains share the same modal model?
    all_same_mode <- n_distinct(mode_summaries$key) == 1
    all_same_mode
    
    # if not, show which chains agree
    split(mode_summaries$chain, mode_summaries$key)
    
    # --- optional: top-K modes per chain --------------------------------------
    
    top_k_modes <- function(chain, K = 5, burn = 1000) {
      #S   <- chain$rs$samples[(burn+1):nrow(chain$rs$samples), , drop = FALSE]
      S   <- chain$rs$samples
      bic <- chain$rs$bic_trace[(burn+1):length(chain$rs$bic_trace)]
      cat(sprintf("nrow(S)=%d, length(bic)=%d\n", nrow(S), length(bic)))
      keys <- apply(S, 1, .bin_key)
      tab  <- sort(table(keys), decreasing = TRUE)
      out_keys <- names(tab)[seq_len(min(K, length(tab)))]
      map_dfr(out_keys, function(k) {
        idx  <- which(keys == k)
        v    <- as.integer(strsplit(k, "")[[1]])
        tibble(
          key = k,
          visits = as.integer(tab[k]),
          prop = as.integer(tab[k]) / nrow(S),
          bic_min = min(bic[idx]),
          bic_median = median(bic[idx]),
          hamming_to_global_best = ham_dist(v,
                                            colnames(X) %in% best_BIC_minimizer_all_lambda$best$v)
        )
      })
    }
    
    top5_by_chain <- imap(chains_075, ~ top_k_modes(.x, K = 5) %>% mutate(chain = .y)) %>% bind_rows()
    top5_by_chain %>% arrange(chain, desc(prop))
}

check_mode_0.75 = check_mode()
check_mode_0.75
```
```{r}
check_mode_0.75 %>% filter(chain == "3: sparse")
```

## IT TURNS OUT SECOND MOST FREQUENT MODEL EXACTLY COINCIDES WITH GLOBAL MINIMIZER!!

```{r}

check_mode_0.75 %>%
  filter(chain == "3: sparse") %>%
  select(visits, prop, bic_min, hamming_to_global_best, chain)

```
```{r}
xtable(
  check_mode_0.75 %>%
  filter(chain == "3: sparse") %>%
  select(visits, prop, bic_min, hamming_to_global_best, chain)
)
```



IT TURNS OUT THE SECOND MOST FREQUENT MODEL MATCHES THE BIC MINMIZER!!!!

```{r}
setdiff(varnames_elites[[1]],
          colnames(X)[sapply(str_split((check_mode_0.75 %>% filter(chain == "3: sparse"))$key[2], "")[[1]], function(x)as.logical(as.integer(x)))])
```
```{r}
setdiff(colnames(X)[sapply(str_split((check_mode_0.75 %>% filter(chain == "3: sparse"))$key[2], "")[[1]], function(x)as.logical(as.integer(x)))],varnames_elites[[1]]
          )
```
